subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the production of magnetic field by electric current
Describe magnetic field lines and patterns around conductors, loops, and solenoids
Apply rules like right-hand thumb rule and Fleming's left-hand rule
Explain working of electric motor and force on current-carrying conductor
Identify applications like electromagnets and domestic wiring safety
Perform simple experiments to observe magnetic effects

lesson_plan.title: Magnetic Field and Oersted's Experiment
topics_covered: Magnetic field and field lines properties
Oersted's discovery: current produces magnetic field
Magnetic field due to straight current-carrying conductor
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of Oersted's experiment with compass and wire
Interactive drawing of field lines on board
student_activities: Observe compass deflection with current ON/OFF
Draw concentric circular field lines for straight wire
Apply right-hand thumb rule to determine direction for 5 cases

lesson_plan.title: Magnetic Field Due to Circular Loop and Solenoid
topics_covered: Magnetic field pattern around current-carrying circular loop
teaching_strategy: Demonstration with coil and iron nail (electromagnet)
Comparison of solenoid field with bar magnet
student_activities: Make a simple solenoid using wire and nail, test with paper clips
Draw field lines for loop and solenoid
List factors affecting solenoid field (current, turns, core)

lesson_plan.title: Force on Current-Carrying Conductor
topics_covered: Force on current-carrying conductor in magnetic field
Fleming's right-hand rule (introduction for generator context)
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of force using current-carrying wire between magnets
Guided application of Fleming's left-hand rule
student_activities: Apply Fleming's left-hand rule to predict direction in 4-5 scenarios
Build a basic model of DC motor using coil, magnet, battery
Observe rotation and explain using rule

lesson_plan.title: Electric Motor and Electromagnet Applications
topics_covered: Working of electric motor (split ring commutator)
teaching_strategy: Group discussion on applications (cranes, MRI, speakers)
student_activities: Disassemble/reassemble toy motor or draw labeled diagram
Solve 5-6 application-based questions on motor direction
Make chart of 6 real-life electromagnet uses

lesson_plan.title: Domestic Electric Circuits and Safety
teaching_strategy: Discussion on common faults and protection
student_activities: Draw labeled domestic circuit diagram with fuse and earthing
Identify safety features in home wiring (survey/note)
Solve questions on why earthing prevents shocks

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Worksheet and Application Questions
topics_covered: Recap of rules, field patterns, motor, circuits
student_activities: Write one application per major concept

teaching_aids: Straight wire, battery, compass, iron filings
Copper coil, iron nail, paper clips (for electromagnet)
Simple DC motor kit or model
teacher_tip: Use plenty of direction-based practice with rules (thumb/fingers) to avoid confusion. Demonstrate every experiment live for safety and clarity. Relate concepts to everyday devices like fans, mixers, and wiring to boost interest and retention.
